sections.instruction: Questions 1-5: Complete the following form with NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Question 6: Mark TWO letters that represent the correct answer. Questions 7-10: Fill in the blanks with NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: Which kind of family does the girl prefer?
options: A. A big family with many young children
B. A family without smokers or drinkers
C. A family without any pets
D. A family with many animals or pets

questions.content: Although the girl is not a vegetarian, she doesn’t eat a lot of meat. Her favourite food is {{BLANK}}.
The girl has given up playing handball. Now, she just plays {{BLANK}} with her friends at weekends.
The girl does not like the bus because they are always late. She would rather {{BLANK}}.
The girl can get the information about the homestay family that she wants {{BLANK}}.

sections.instruction: Complete the notes by filling in the blanks with NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
NOTES
VISAS
Now Eznina visas are needed by almost all nationalities.
Normal visas last {{BLANK}}
You need to pay {{BLANK}} for the visa.
Price may change from time to time.
Some Enzian consulates in neighbouring countries require you to provide a letter to {{BLANK}}.
You can get information of major embassies on {{BLANK}} of the student handbook.
If you want to re-enter Eznia, you must get a multi-entry visa.

SOME MISCELLANEOUS GENERAL ADVICE
If you carry a lot of money, you need to complete a {{BLANK}}.
Remember to declare all your items, especially expensive items, on a {{BLANK}}.
You are advised to carry a health certificate. The one you need is the {{BLANK}}.

NOTES FOR STUDENTS
If you wish to get a youth fare card, you should show your {{BLANK}}.
Due to the bureaucracy in Eznia, you are advised to take at least {{BLANK}} passport photos with you.

CURRENCY
Pounds and US dollars are not very useful now in Eznia, so you should take {{BLANK}} or {{BLANK}} with you.

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 25-27: Fill in the blanks with ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 28-30: Mark THREE letters that represent the correct answers.

questions.content: The woman being interviewed is now working in the bank. Her occupation is {{BLANK}}.
The woman usually spends about {{BLANK}} when she goes shopping.
The woman often goes to {{BLANK}} because she finds them convenient.
According to the woman, {{BLANK}} is/are her most difficult thing(s) to buy.
50% of the people being interviewed spend {{BLANK}} a month.
15% of the people being interviewed spend {{BLANK}} a month.
35% of the people being interviewed spend {{BLANK}} a month.
Most of the people being interviewed think that {{BLANK}} is/are most difficult to buy.

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.

questions.content: What does the lecturer provide for those who are interested in doing extra reading?
options: B. Extra materials, such as a booklist.

questions.content: In the past, time management meant you needed to
options: B. plan for every hour of the week.
D. set goals and try to achieve these goals.

questions.content: Today, wise time management means you need to
options: A. set goals and work in a systematic way.
C. set an overview of your assignment.
D. make a list, plan for everything and try to stick to this plan.

questions.content: In this college, students are assigned ____________ at the end of each semester.
One sign the lecturer mentions that students feel under pressure is
options: B. students get angry for no reason.

questions.content: What kind of suggestion does the lecturer give to the students?
options: A. Making a very detailed plan of their daily activities.
B. Not being so stressed just because there is an assignment.
C. A regular one-hour session in their personal timetables.

questions.content: According to the lecturer, there are three kinds of planners. They are:
options: A. one weekly planner, one daily planner and one hour planner.
B. one yearly planner, one weekly planner and one daily planner.
C. one term planner, one monthly planner and one weekly planner.
D. one term planner, one weekly planner and one daily planner.

questions.content: If you want to set an overview of your time, you should need at least
The daily planner of time is mainly concerned with
options: B. how to plan all available time.
D. an overview of everything you need to do for several days.

questions.content: According to the lecturer, wise time management may have the following benefit:
options: A. having more time to spend on relaxation and other activities.
B. improving your performance in the final term assignment.
C. helping you write better essays.
